title: Learnable Scale & Shift
concept: After normalizing activations, batch norm adds learnable controls that let the network decide how much to stretch or shift those values to keep useful patterns.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the first job of batch normalization?

options.text: To recenter and rescale hidden activations so the next layer sees a steadier range.
To erase the need for hidden layers.
To randomize the labels in every batch.

exercises.explain: Correct. Batch norm first normalizes the hidden activations seen by the next layer.

cards.title: Auto-correct, then fine-tune
cards.body: Many photo apps first auto-balance an image so it is easier to work with. But they still let you change brightness and contrast afterward. Batch norm works that way too: it tidies the activations, then lets the network decide the most useful final look.

cards.title: Normalization with adjustable knobs
cards.body: Batch norm does not lock activations into one fixed shape forever. After centering and scaling them, it includes learnable scale and shift parameters. These let the network stretch the values wider, squeeze them tighter, or slide them up and down if that helps learning.

cards.title: A mixing board after noise cleanup
cards.body: Imagine a sound engineer first removes wild volume swings, then uses sliders to boost the bass or soften the treble. Cleaning the signal does not remove artistic control. In the same way, batch norm cleans the activation range but still gives the network tuning knobs.

cards.title: A feature needs to sit higher
cards.body: Suppose a later layer learns best when a certain feature is usually a little positive instead of centered right around zero. Batch norm can normalize that feature first, then learn to shift it upward again. The layer gets stability without losing flexibility.

cards.title: Normalization does not erase expressiveness
cards.body: A common fear is that normalized activations become too restricted to represent rich patterns. That would be true if the values were frozen forever. But batch norm adds learnable scale and shift, so the network can still shape the representation it wants.

left.text:
Values are cleaned up.
But the network must accept one default range.
Less freedom to shape features.

right.text:
Values are cleaned up first.
Then the network can stretch or slide them.
Stability and flexibility both remain.

exercises.prompt: Why does batch norm include learnable scale and shift after normalization?

options.text: So the network can restore or reshape useful activation ranges instead of being stuck with one fixed normalized form.
So it can remove all gradients from the layer.
So it can swap the input data with hidden activations.
So it can force every feature to remain exactly zero forever.

exercises.explain: Exactly. The extra learned knobs keep batch norm flexible instead of rigid.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Batch norm always forces the final activations to stay in one permanently fixed shape with no learned adjustment.
exercises.explain: False. Batch norm normalizes first, then learns how much to stretch and shift the values afterward.

exercises.sentence: Batch norm keeps flexibility by learning how to stretch and ___ the normalized values.
exercises.explain: Right. The learned shift lets the network move the activations up or down after normalization.

exercises.prompt: Which situation best shows learnable scale and shift?

options.text: A feature is normalized for stability, then the network learns to make it a bit larger and more positive because that range helps the next layer.
A batch is normalized, so the model is never allowed to change that feature again.
A GPU changes its screen brightness while training.

exercises.explain: Yes. Batch norm stabilizes the feature first, then learns the most useful final scale and position.

exercises.prompt: An engineer worries that batch normalization will wash out a useful feature detector because all activations get normalized. What is the best response?

options.text: Batch norm normalizes first for stability, but it also learns how much to stretch and shift the feature afterward if that helps the network.
Batch norm permanently freezes every feature at zero, so the engineer is correct.
Batch norm only works because it removes all hidden-layer meaning.
Batch norm replaces learned features with random noise after each batch.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Batch norm is not a straightjacket; it is a stabilization step with learnable tuning knobs built in. Next, we'll look at a practical twist: batch norm behaves differently while training than it does during prediction time.
